description: Multi-DB Key Generator system with Admin Panel, User Panel, and Mobile App integration. Keys are generated via web panels and consumed by a mobile app for device-based access control.

architecture.overview: 3-tier system: User Panel (web) generates keys → Admin Panel (web) manages everything → Mobile App (Android) consumes keys with device binding

user_panel.description: Public-facing key generator page. Users access via secret URL parameter.

admin_panel.description: Full management dashboard for admin. Firebase Auth required.
admin_panel.access: URL query parameter (default: ?admin=true) → login page with email/password
features: Keyboard shortcuts (1-7 for pages, R for refresh)

mobile_app.description: Android app that verifies keys and binds them to devices. NOT part of this codebase — built separately.
responsibilities: App reads key from Firebase ActiveUserKeys
If key exists and device matches or is unbound → grant access
First login: set boundDeviceId = device's hardware ID, isUsed = true
Subsequent logins: verify boundDeviceId matches current device

ActiveUserKeys.description: All generated keys stored here. Both panels and mobile app read/write this.

durationHours.description: Key validity in hours. 99999 = lifetime (never expires)

isUsed.description: false = never used. true = bound to a device. Set by mobile app on first login.

boundDeviceId.description: Device hardware ID that this key is locked to. 'NONE' = unbound.

note.description: Admin or user alias for the key

ActiveUserKeys.write_rules: public (auth not required) — user panel has no auth

SystemSettings.description: Global configuration for the entire system

cooldownHours.description: Hours before a user can generate new keys after hitting the limit

maxKeysLimit.description: Maximum keys a single user can generate within cooldown window

maintenanceMode.description: When true, non-admin users see maintenance screen. Admins bypass this.

userParam.description: URL query parameter for user panel access

adminParam.description: URL query parameter for admin panel access

defaultKeyDuration.description: Default key validity in hours when generated from admin panel

defaultKeyTier.description: Default key tier for admin panel generation

defaultKeyLifetime.description: When true, admin-generated keys get duration 99999 (lifetime)

lastNotifReadTime.description: Timestamp of last notification read by admin (for badge count)

SystemStats.description: Lifetime statistics and daily generation counts

DailyGenerations.description: Key-value pairs where key is YYYY-MM-DD and value is count

ConnectedFirebases.description: Mirror/external Firebase databases for multi-DB sync

BannedDevices.description: List of banned device IDs. Mobile app checks this before granting access.
BannedDevices.read_rules: public (mobile app needs to check before access)

AdminLogs.description: Audit log of all admin actions with undo capability

type.description: Action type for filtering and undo logic

undoData.description: Data needed to reverse this action

undone.description: true if this action was undone

AdminNotifications.description: Persisted notification feed for admin panel

multi_db_sync.description: When admin generates a key, it is written to ALL connected mirror databases AND the main DB. User panel reads from mirror DBs. This ensures redundancy.
multi_db_sync.write_order: Mirror DBs first, then main DB
multi_db_sync.read_behavior: All DBs merged into unified view. Device binding from any DB is respected.

generation.admin_panel: Admin chooses tier (Normal/VIP), duration, optional note → clicks Generate & Sync → key created on all DBs
generation.user_panel: User opens page with correct URL param → key auto-generated with default settings from SystemSettings
generation.key_format: PREFIX-XXXXXX where PREFIX is 'PH' (normal) or 'VIP', and XXXXXX is random alphanumeric (A-Z, 0-9)

active_usage.step_1: User enters key in mobile app
active_usage.step_3: If key does not exist → 'Invalid Key' error
active_usage.step_4: If key exists, check BannedDevices for boundDeviceId → if banned → 'Access Denied'
active_usage.step_5: If key.isUsed == false (first time) → grant access, set boundDeviceId = device hardware ID, isUsed = true
active_usage.step_6: If key.isUsed == true AND boundDeviceId == current device → grant access
active_usage.step_7: If key.isUsed == true AND boundDeviceId != current device → 'Key bound to another device' error
active_usage.step_8: If current time > createdAt + (durationHours * 3600000) → key expired, reject

expiration.auto_cleanup: Admin panel has auto-cleanup that removes expired keys from all DBs
expiration.user_panel_cleanup: User panel removes expired keys from its local view after 2 seconds

admin_operations.edit: Change duration of any key (applied to all DBs)
admin_operations.delete: Remove key from all DBs permanently
admin_operations.unbind: Reset boundDeviceId to 'NONE' and isUsed to false — key can be used on a new device
admin_operations.ban: Add device to BannedDevices + delete the key
admin_operations.undo: Reverse any of the above operations using stored undoData

user_panel.method: No authentication. Access via secret URL parameter only.
user_panel.bypass: Admin URL param (e.g. ?admin=true) bypasses cooldown limits
user_panel.security: URL is cleaned from address bar after access (history.replaceState)

login_flow: 7. If not authenticated → redirect back to login
8. Session stored in sessionStorage (30 min timer)

session_management.auto_logout: Redirect to login page when timer expires (client-side, not enforced server-side)

admin_panel.rate_limiting: Login page has attempt counter. After 5 failed attempts, 30 second cooldown.

mobile_app.method: No Firebase Auth. Device binding IS the authentication.
mobile_app.trust_model: Anyone with a valid key can use it. Security comes from 1-key-1-device binding.

device_binding.description: Each key can only be used on one device. First device to use the key gets it locked.

fields.boundDeviceId: Hardware ID of the device using this key. 'NONE' if unbound.
fields.isUsed: boolean. true = bound to a device.

flow.first_use: App sets boundDeviceId = device_hardware_id, isUsed = true
flow.subsequent_uses: App checks if boundDeviceId matches current device_hardware_id
flow.mismatch: If boundDeviceId != current device → reject with 'Key bound to another device'
flow.admin_unbind: Admin can reset boundDeviceId to 'NONE' — key becomes free to use on any device

ban_flow.admin_bans_device: 1. Add device to BannedDevices path, 2. Delete the key from ActiveUserKeys
ban_flow.app_check: App reads BannedDevices/<device_id>. If exists → reject access.
ban_flow.unban: Admin can undo ban (restores key + removes from BannedDevices)

cooldown_system.description: Prevents abuse by limiting key generation frequency
cooldown_system.storage: localStorage key: 'ph_gen_timestamps' — array of Date.now() timestamps
logic: 1. On key generation, push current timestamp to array
2. If array length >= maxKeysLimit → block generation, show cooldown timer
4. When oldest timestamp is older than cooldown → clear array, allow generation

admin_panel.sidebar_navigation: 7 pages: Dashboard, Generator, Key Manager, Active Devices, Firebase Hub, Security, Settings
admin_panel.notifications: Real-time notification bell with unread count, persisted to Firebase
admin_panel.activity_log: Every admin action logged with undo capability

user_panel.key_dashboard: Shows generated keys with live status (Unused/Active/Expired)

mobile_app_integration_guide.description: Instructions for building the mobile app that consumes these keys

firebase_setup.step_1: Use the SAME firebase-config.js values (apiKey, databaseURL, projectId, etc.)
firebase_setup.step_3: No Firebase Auth needed — read ActiveUserKeys directly (rules allow public read)

key_verification_algorithm.pseudocode:
FUNCTION verifyKey(inputKey, deviceId):
  keyRef = GET ActiveUserKeys/{inputKey}
  
  IF keyRef NOT EXISTS:
    RETURN 'Invalid Key'
  
  keyData = keyRef.value
  
  // Check if banned
  banRef = GET BannedDevices/{deviceId}
  IF banRef EXISTS:
    RETURN 'Device Banned'
  
  // Check expiry
  currentTime = NOW
  expiryTime = keyData.createdAt + (keyData.durationHours * 3600000)
  IF keyData.durationHours != 99999 AND currentTime > expiryTime:
    DELETE ActiveUserKeys/{inputKey}  // cleanup
    RETURN 'Key Expired'
  
  // Check device binding
  IF keyData.isUsed == false:
    // First use — bind device
    UPDATE ActiveUserKeys/{inputKey}:
      boundDeviceId = deviceId
      isUsed = true
    RETURN 'Access Granted (New Device)'
  
  IF keyData.boundDeviceId == deviceId:
    RETURN 'Access Granted'
  
  ELSE:
    RETURN 'Key Bound to Another Device'

device_id_requirements.description: Must be a unique, persistent hardware identifier
device_id_requirements.recommended: Android Settings.Secure.ANDROID_ID or a combination of hardware serial + brand
device_id_requirements.must_be: Consistent across app reinstalls, non-changeable by user

Admin-Key-Generators/Admin-Key-Generator.admin-script.js: All admin logic (key mgmt, settings, notifications, multi-DB)
Admin-Key-Generators/Admin-Key-Generator.admin-style.css: Admin panel styles with responsive design + animations
Admin-Key-Generators/Admin-Key-Generator.firebase-rules.json: Clean RTDB rules for Firebase Console paste

Key-Generators/Key-Generators-main/.script.js: Key generation, cooldown, dashboard, live sync
Key-Generators/Key-Generators-main/.style.css: User panel styles with theme toggle
